title: galium aparine proles spurium
intro: Galium aparine proles spurium, also known as cleavers, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae and is characterized by its climbing habit and sticky stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of cleavers include its ability to stimulate the lymphatic system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. Cleavers can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
